steps.title: Identify that both equations represent the same line
steps.content:
The second equation is 6 times the first:

$$6(5x + 7y) = 6(8) \Rightarrow 30x + 42y = 48$$

Since the equations are identical, the system has infinitely many solutions—all points on the line $5x + 7y = 8$.

steps.title: Parametrize the line by setting x = r
steps.content:
Let $x = r$ (any real number). Substitute into the line equation:

$$5r + 7y = 8$$
$$y = -\frac{5r}{7} + \frac{8}{7}$$

So the point $\left(r, -\frac{5r}{7} + \frac{8}{7}\right)$ satisfies both equations for any real $r$. This matches choice A — A is correct.
